title: Broadcast and Event Propagation

activities.name: _init
steps: For each peer_config: resolvePeerId → if duplicate warn → store in #peersByKey
If peer has webhook=true → push to #webhookPeers; else warn (websocket disabled)

activities.name: #initRedisPubSub
steps: Check redisClient.duplicate is available; if not → warn and return

activities.name: outBroadcastEventHandler (triggered by outer.* event)
steps: Build outboundEvent = { key, data, meta: safeMeta }
Call #publishWebhookEventsToRedis([outboundEvent]) (async, fire-and-forget with warning on error)

activities.name: #enqueueOutboundEvent
steps: Create dedupKey = JSON.stringify(event) OR fallback counter string

activities.name: #flushOutboundEvents
steps: [Guard] if #outboundIsFlushing OR no queued events → return
Drain: collect all events from #outboundEventsByDedupKey, clear map
For each peer in #webhookPeers → await #sendWebhookToPeer(peer, events) [catch per-peer errors]
[Condition] if #outboundEventsByDedupKey still has events → re-schedule flush

activities.name: #sendWebhookToPeer
steps: Resolve peerId and webhook_url; if missing → return
Get mySecretKey from config.webhook.secret; if missing → return
Build headers: Content-Type, Content-Length, X-Broadcast-Peer-Id, X-Broadcast-Timestamp, X-Broadcast-Nonce, X-Broadcast-Signature, [Host]
[Condition] if response.status not 2xx → throw Error (logged as warning by caller)

activities.name: #publishWebhookEventsToRedis
steps: Filter events: only outer.pub or outer.pub.* keys
Build JSON payload: { sourceId: #redisSourceId, events: filteredEvents }

activities.name: #handleRedisPubSubMessage (incoming from Redis)
steps: Parse JSON message; invalid → warn and return

activities.name: #handleWebhookRequest (POST /broadcast/webhook)
steps: [Validate] peer exists in #peersByKey with webhook_secret → else 403
[Validate] timestamp within replay window (now - window to now + 60s) → else 400

activities.name: #emitIncomingEventsSequentially
steps: For each event: [Guard] if event.meta.from_outside → warn + skip

key_relationships: BroadcastService publishes to Redis PubSub channel (broadcast.webhook.events)
BroadcastService subscribes to Redis PubSub via duplicate client
BroadcastService emits back into EventService with from_outside=true flag
BroadcastService POSTs to configured peer webhook URLs via axios
flow_decision_points: peer.webhook === true → include in webhookPeers, else skip (websocket disabled)
Redis available → use PubSub; else skip Redis path
Dedup key collision → overwrite (deduplicate duplicate events)
#outboundIsFlushing → skip flush (prevent concurrent flushes), re-schedule if needed
timestamp out of window → reject webhook
important_notes: Outbound events are batched: deduped by JSON.stringify key, flushed after #outboundFlushMs (default 5s)
Redis PubSub used for same-cluster instance fan-out (outer.pub events only)
Webhook transport uses HMAC-SHA256 with per-peer monotonic nonces + timestamp for anti-replay
from_outside flag on meta prevents re-emission loops across cluster nodes
Route: POST /broadcast/webhook registered via eggspress on app
